title: COUNTIFS
summary: COUNTIFS counts how many rows meet one or more criteria across columns. It is essential for building dashboards, summaries, and frequency counts.
jobUseCases: Counting how many employees belong to a specific department and location
Tallying the number of orders above a certain value for a sales report
Counting open support tickets assigned to a particular agent
Measuring how many candidates passed each round in a hiring funnel
Counting transactions that fall within a chosen date range

examples.title: Count one category
examples.explanation: Counts how many cells in A2:A100 contain the department 'Sales'. With a single condition it behaves like COUNTIF.

examples.title: Two conditions
examples.explanation: Counts employees who are in the 'Sales' department AND based in 'Mumbai'. A row is counted only when both conditions are TRUE.

examples.title: Numeric threshold
examples.explanation: Counts how many orders in C2:C100 exceed 50,000. The operator and value go together inside one set of quotes.

examples.title: Count within a date range
examples.explanation: Counts records dated in January 2026 by referencing the same date column twice with two boundary conditions.

commonMistakes: Confusing COUNTIFS (count rows) with SUMIFS (add values); COUNTIFS has no sum_range
Giving criteria ranges of different lengths, which triggers a #VALUE! error
Forgetting to join operators with &, e.g. ">=D2" instead of ">="&D2
Expecting OR logic when COUNTIFS applies AND across all criteria pairs
Counting blank cells unexpectedly; use "<>" to count non-blanks or "" to count blanks
practiceTips: Use COUNTIFS to power dashboard KPI tiles like 'Open Tickets' or 'Deals Won'
Combine with wildcards (* and ?) to count partial text matches
Reference dropdown cells as criteria to make counts interactive
